SEARCH
Package fr.sorbonne_u.cps.pubsub.demo

Class DemoTimedDistributed

java.lang.Object
fr.sorbonne_u.components.cvm.AbstractCVM
fr.sorbonne_u.components.cvm.AbstractDistributedCVM
fr.sorbonne_u.cps.pubsub.demo.DemoTimedDistributed
All Implemented Interfaces:
fr.sorbonne_u.components.cvm.ComponentVirtualMachineI, fr.sorbonne_u.components.cvm.DistributedComponentVirtualMachineI
public class DemoTimedDistributed extends fr.sorbonne_u.components.cvm.AbstractDistributedCVM
Distributed CVM demonstrating the gossip protocol between two brokers across two separate JVMs.

Description

This deployment validates the gossip protocol (CDC §3.7) by running two broker components on separate JVMs, each hosting one client component:

  • JVM "broker1-jvm" — Broker B1 + SubscriberClient C1 (FREE class) + ClocksServer
  • JVM "broker2-jvm" — Broker B2 + PrivilegedClient C2 (starts FREE, upgrades to STANDARD)

Test scenario

  1. C1 and C2 register at their respective local brokers; registration is propagated via RegisterGossipMessage.
  2. C2 upgrades to STANDARD via modifyServiceClass.
  3. C2 creates a privileged channel "gossip-channel" restricted to C1; creation is propagated via CreateChannelGossipMessage so B1 replicates the channel locally.
  4. C1 subscribes to "gossip-channel" on B1.
  5. C2 publishes a message on "gossip-channel" via B2; B2 validates and propagates a PublishGossipMessage to B1, which delivers the message to C1.

Invariant

 invariant true

      public DemoTimedDistributed(String[] args) throws Exception
      Instantiate the distributed CVM object.

      Contract

       pre args != null && args.length >= 2
 post true
      Parameters:
      args - command-line arguments: args[0] is the URI of this JVM in the assembly; args[1] is the path to the XML configuration file.
      Throws:
      Exception - if the superclass constructor fails.

      Throws:
      Exception

    • testScenario

      public static fr.sorbonne_u.components.utils.tests.TestScenario testScenario() throws Exception
      Build the timed test scenario shared by all client components.

      Steps (all instants relative to "2026-02-01T10:00:00.00Z", real-time with ACCELERATION_FACTOR = 1.0):

      1. t+5 s — C2 upgrades to STANDARD.
      2. t+15 s — C2 creates privileged channel "gossip-channel"; gossip propagates to B1.
      3. t+25 s — C1 subscribes to "gossip-channel" on B1 (channel is now known locally via gossip).
      4. t+35 s — C2 publishes a message; B2 sends a PublishGossipMessage to B1 which delivers it to C1.
      Returns:
      the fully configured TestScenario.
      Throws:
      Exception - if scenario construction fails.

    • acceptAll

      protected static MessageFilterI acceptAll()
      Build a message filter that accepts every message.
      Returns:
      a MessageFilter with no property or time constraints.

    • instantiateAndPublish

      public void instantiateAndPublish() throws Exception
      Instantiate and publish components on the current JVM.

      On "broker1-jvm": creates Broker B1 (gossip neighbour = B2), SubscriberClient C1, and the shared ClocksServer.
      On "broker2-jvm": creates Broker B2 (gossip neighbour = B1) and PrivilegedClient C2.

      Specified by:
      instantiateAndPublish in interface fr.sorbonne_u.components.cvm.DistributedComponentVirtualMachineI
      Overrides:
      instantiateAndPublish in class fr.sorbonne_u.components.cvm.AbstractDistributedCVM
      Throws:
      Exception - if component creation or port publication fails.

    • interconnect

      public void interconnect() throws Exception
      Specified by:
      interconnect in interface fr.sorbonne_u.components.cvm.DistributedComponentVirtualMachineI
      Overrides:
      interconnect in class fr.sorbonne_u.components.cvm.AbstractDistributedCVM
      Throws:
      Exception

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      Entry point.

      Starts the standard BCM4Java life cycle with a total budget of 120 seconds (15000L ms startup delay + 100 s scenario + safety margin).

      Parameters:
      args - JVM URI and config file path (forwarded to the constructor).
